summary:
- **Core topic/purpose:** Petition by **Messrs. Alkaff & Company** (Singapore agents) on behalf of **Hadramaut-based Arab property owners/trustees (Alkaff family)** seeking **revocation of a Governor-in-Council order** laying out a **new “back lane”/street** that would require acquisition of parts of their property and, they argue, harm future development.  
- **Geography & period:** Concerns **Singapore (Straits Settlements)**, specifically property at/around **Anson Road, Trafalgar Street, Selangor Street**; events span **1934–1940** (petition dated **2 April 1940**; Colonial Office handling noted **July–Aug 1940**).  
- **Key actors:** Petitioners/owners include **Syed Aboobakar, Syed Abdulrahman, Syed Omar, Syed Abdulgader, Syed Hassan bin Shaikh Alkaff** (trustees of a **6 March 1924** charitable settlement); **Singapore Improvement Trust (SIT)** (manager **L. Langdon Williams**; acting manager **James M. Fraser**); enquiry officers **W.S. Ebden** (first) and **L.H. Gorsuch** (second); solicitors **Sisson & Delay** (owners) and **Braddell Brothers** (SIT); referenced neighbor **Fraser & Neave Ltd.**  
- **Major events/decisions (first scheme):** SIT applied **26 Apr 1934**; Governor-in-Council ordered back lane **7 Nov 1934** (Gazette **No. 2614**, published **16 Nov 1934**; registered **14 Dec 1934**, Vol. 865 Nos. 114–115). This order was later **quashed by the Court of Appeal on 9 Aug 1937** via **certiorari**, because the enquiry officer (**Commissioner of Lands**, also an SIT member) was held **disqualified due to suspicion of legal bias**.  
- **Major events/decisions (second scheme):** SIT renewed application (gazetted **22 Oct 1937**, Gazette **No. 3047**); second enquiry held beginning **22 Mar 1938**; despite objections, Governor-in-Council made a **majority decision order on 7 Sep 1938**, published **16 Sep 1938** (Gazette **No. 2712**)—reported as **identical/practically similar** to the earlier scheme.  
- **Petitioners’ main arguments & proposals:** They sought an **alternative back lane alignment** (alongside **Fraser & Neave’s** premises) to avoid splitting their block into multiple uneconomic parcels; argued the Trust’s siting would reduce depths of some lots (figures cited include **~95–107 ft** for Anson Road lots; **~62–66 ft** for certain Trafalgar/Selangor lots depending on lane width), impede redevelopment, and impose unnecessary public cost (they cite **<$6,000** vs Trust’s believed **$20,000**). They also emphasized planned improvements submitted **18 Oct 1937** (kitchens/bathrooms/WCs; demolition of Trafalgar/Selangor houses to create open space).  
- **Outcome/official response (Colonial Office):** The Colonial Office dispatch (to **Lord Lloyd of Dolobran**) states the order is a **quasi-judicial act**, so a request for directions to revoke/substitute it **“cannot be entertained”**; given the order followed **public enquiry** and normal town-planning practice, the recommendation was to **decline to intervene** (internal note dated **15 Aug 1940**).
